authoritative_sources.title: General Compliance Program Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG is the federal oversight authority for healthcare fraud, waste, abuse, exclusions, and compliance guidance; the GCPG is OIG's central public healthcare compliance program reference.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Compliance program infrastructure, risk assessment, policies, reporting, investigations, auditing, monitoring, and remediation
used_for: policy, training, hotline, audit, monitoring, and remediation workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for State Medicaid Agencies: Exclusions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG administers LEIE and this official guidance identifies monthly exclusion-check expectations and OIG database evidence sources.

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of the HIPAA Privacy Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR implements and enforces HIPAA Privacy Rule requirements for covered entities and business associates, PHI, uses and disclosures, individual rights, and administrative safeguards.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-sensitive evidence handling and privacy escalation
used_for: privacy officer and breach signoff boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA enforcement authority and this official guidance constrains routine and non-routine PHI uses, disclosures, and requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI minimization, access-log review, and evidence packet preparation
used_for: PHI redaction and approved retrieval hooks
refusal when unnecessary PHI is pasted into unapproved channels

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Review and Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare medical review and identifies review documentation, claim-review source hierarchy, NCD/LCD/manual use, and medical review contractor operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Billing audit evidence, claim review, NCD/LCD/manual source hierarchy, and payer boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the Medicare payer authority and the manual chapters define benefits, provider relationships, organization determinations, grievances, appeals, sanctions, and compliance program expectations for managed care.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Managed-care eligibility, authorization, organization determination, appeal, provider relationship, and compliance program dependencies
used_for: member, claim, appeal, and provider-record prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: Measures Management System: Measure Lifecycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Measures Management System is CMS's official public measure development and maintenance reference and is authoritative for measure lifecycle, measured entity, data-element, and quality-reporting terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality-measure evidence, data-element traceability, and reporting workflow

authoritative_sources.title: AHRQ Common Formats - Comprehensive Patient Safety Reporting Using AHRQ Common Formats
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS survey guidance ties AHRQ Common Formats to hospital QAPI adverse-event tracking expectations, making it authoritative for patient-safety reporting workflow and escalation terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient-safety event tracking, QAPI linkage, and safety escalation
used_for: adverse event, near miss, and unsafe condition terminology
refusal to close clinical harm as documentation-only

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and regulated context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, tenant policies, jurisdiction, business unit, facility, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, patient/member status, service dates, authorization, referral, consent, PHI handling constraints, systems of record, and owner matrix before producing compliance outputs.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Obligation, policy, and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map OIG, CMS, OCR, state regulator, payer, accreditation, and tenant obligations to controls, evidence artifacts, evidence owners, review cadence, and escalation triggers.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Healthcare evidence assembly and source-of-truth review
workflow_stages.description: Validate policies, audit plans, monitoring logs, hotline records, exclusion checks, training attestations, billing audit findings, EHR and claim records, authorization/referral records, access logs, committee materials, and CAPA artifacts against named systems of record.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Finding classification and boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify findings as documentation gaps, control failures, possible privacy matters, potential billing/fraud/legal issues, payer-interpretation blockers, or clinical/patient-safety signals requiring escalation.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Remediation tracking and review preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence packets, compliance finding summaries, remediation owner trackers, compliance committee materials, review or attestation preparation packets, and assumption logs without claiming legal, clinical, payer, privacy, or regulator signoff.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, refusal, and adjacent-owner handoff
workflow_stages.description: Refuse or escalate legal advice, regulator negotiation, self-disclosure decisions, payer interpretation, coding selection, privacy breach signoff, fraud conclusions, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment, level-of-care, and patient safety resolution while preserving evidence for authorized owners.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Compliance policies, policy hierarchy, approval matrix, compliance committee charter, audit plan, annual work plan, risk assessment, and regulator/payer source references
Monitoring logs, audit workpapers, hotline reports, incident reports, investigation intake records, corrective-action plans, retest evidence, and remediation closure approvals
Training attestations, policy acknowledgments, exclusion checks, sanction-screening results, roster extracts, vendor/provider records, and follow-up evidence for potential matches

EHR extracts, encounter records, orders, clinical documentation references, billing records, claim records, denial records, authorization records, referral records, payer portal evidence, NCD/LCD/manual references, and medical review documentation requests
Access logs, break-glass logs, privacy case records, consent records, minimum-necessary review notes, role-access approvals, retention and legal-hold flags, and PHI redaction logs
Quality measure specifications, measure data-element maps, QAPI records, adverse-event or near-miss reports, unsafe-condition reports, patient-safety escalation records, and committee materials
systems_of_record: Compliance case management or GRC system
Billing, claims, denial, and revenue integrity systems
Payer portal, authorization, referral, and eligibility systems
Identity, access-log, break-glass, and SIEM systems used for healthcare access evidence
Provider, vendor, HR, credentialing, and sanction-screening systems
Quality reporting, QAPI, patient-safety, and measure data systems
Document repository with retention, legal-hold, and audit trail controls

allowed: Classify healthcare compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Map obligations to controls, evidence owners, review cadence, and systems of record.
Determine whether visible evidence is complete, missing, stale, conflicting, untraceable, or blocked by required owner review.
Prepare compliance finding summaries, remediation trackers, evidence checklists, and escalation notes for authorized owners.
escalate: Tenant policy, jurisdiction, program, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, patient/member status, service date, authorization, referral, coverage, consent, or minimum-necessary handling facts are missing.
EHR, billing, claim, authorization, referral, access-log, training, policy, audit, or compliance case records conflict or cannot be tied to a system of record.
Legal, fraud, privacy officer, regulator-facing, payer interpretation, coding, billing, utilization management, clinical leadership, or patient-safety authority is required.
Operational practice conflicts with tenant policy, payer guidance, public regulator guidance, or state/facility requirements.
refuse: Request seeks legal advice, privileged investigation conclusions, self-disclosure decisions, direct regulator negotiation, or formal certification.
Request asks the lane to sign off that a billing, privacy, fraud, overpayment, payer, or regulator-sensitive issue is legally resolved.
Request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, level-of-care, clinical appropriateness, discharge safety, or patient-safety resolution.
Request exposes unnecessary PHI in an unapproved channel or asks for PHI handling outside approved role, purpose, consent, or minimum-necessary limits.

domain_failure_modes: Producing a generic compliance checklist that ignores healthcare-specific records such as EHR, claims, authorizations, referrals, access logs, PHI controls, exclusion checks, medical review requests, QAPI records, and patient-safety reports.
Closing a compliance finding when audit evidence is incomplete, stale, not tied to a system of record, or missing owner approval.
Treating tenant policy as sufficient when payer authorization, referral, eligibility, coverage, or documentation requirements materially gate the workflow.
Exposing PHI or privacy-sensitive details outside approved retrieval, role-access, consent, or minimum-necessary constraints.
Resolving conflicts among OIG/CMS/OCR guidance, payer policy, state rule, tenant policy, and operational practice without authorized precedence.
Providing legal, fraud, privacy breach, payer, coding, billing, regulator-facing, or clinical conclusions outside lane authority.
Misclassifying patient-safety or clinical-risk signals as simple documentation gaps and failing to route to licensed or safety owners.
Assuming one healthcare compliance baseline applies equally across facility types, provider types, payer products, jurisdictions, and service settings without tenant-specific facts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Audit evidence is incomplete for a required control
Policy language conflicts with operational practice or payer guidance
A possible compliance issue crosses into legal or fraud territory
Request for formal legal interpretation or signoff outside lane authority
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Healthcare compliance obligations vary by state, facility type, payer, provider type, service setting, and tenant policy. The pack is validated because it requires runtime retrieval and escalation rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific authorization, referral, coverage, coding, and medical review requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public CMS sources provide the baseline, but tenant payer contracts and current plan guidance must be retrieved before case-specific conclusions.
The lane may identify evidence gaps and route safety signals, but licensed clinical or patient-safety owners decide clinical adequacy, harm, causation, and resolution.

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Tenant policy hierarchy, jurisdiction, payer or plan, patient/member eligibility, service dates, referral status, authorization status, coverage rule reference, provider type, facility type, service setting, consent, minimum-necessary PHI handling, and approved retrieval hooks must be known before case-specific output.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR encounter records, billing and claim systems, payer portal, authorization and referral systems, eligibility system, access logs, compliance case management, policy management, training, audit-tracking, privacy case management, and document repositories establish the truth set.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at evidence handling, gap classification, remediation tracking, and escalation. Licensed clinical owners decide medical necessity, treatment, level of care, clinical appropriateness, and patient safety; privacy owners decide breach or violation conclusions; payer/revenue integrity/coding/billing owners decide coverage, authorization, coding, utilization, and claim-payment interpretation.
